tools.name: search
tools.description:
Search arXiv papers by title, keywords, or arXiv ID.

Args:
    query: Search query (title, keywords, or arXiv ID like 2401.12345)
    max_results: Maximum number of results to return (default: 10)

Returns:
    List of matching papers with ID, title, authors, and abstract

tools.name: get_paper
tools.description:
Get the full text of an arXiv paper.

Args:
    paper_id: arXiv paper ID (e.g., "2401.12345")
    section: Which section to return: "all", "abstract", "introduction", "method", "conclusion"

Returns:
    The paper text (full or specified section)

tools.name: download_paper
tools.description:
Download arXiv paper PDF to local filesystem.

Args:
    paper_id: arXiv paper ID (e.g., "2401.12345")
    target_dir: Optional target directory. If not specified, uses default storage.

Returns:
    Path to the downloaded PDF file

tools.name: list_downloaded_papers
tools.description:
List all locally downloaded papers.

Returns:
    List of downloaded papers with their metadata

findings.title: Parameter 'target_dir' in 'download_paper' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'target_dir'.

findings.title: Slow tool response — 'download_paper' took 13.4s
findings.description: Tool 'download_paper' took 13.4 seconds to respond. Slow responses can be used for resource exhaustion in agent pipelines or indicate unbounded computation triggered by benign inputs.
findings.remediation: Enforce server-side timeouts on all tool executions. Cap at 30s max.

findings.title: SSRF (timeout) — 'download_paper.paper_id' hung on SSRF payload
findings.description: Call with SSRF payload 'http://169.254.169.254/' timed out after 12.5s, suggesting the server is attempting the network request.
findings.remediation: Block outbound requests to RFC-1918 and link-local ranges. Use an allowlist for permitted URL schemes and hosts.

findings.title: SSRF (timeout) — 'download_paper.target_dir' hung on SSRF payload
findings.description: Call with SSRF payload 'http://169.254.169.254/metadata/instance?api-version=2021-02-01' timed out after 12.0s, suggesting the server is attempting the network request.
findings.remediation: Block outbound requests to RFC-1918 and link-local ranges. Use an allowlist for permitted URL schemes and hosts.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 3 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Protocol crash — JSON-RPC batch array caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a JSON-RPC 2.0 batch (5 requests as an array) caused the server to crash or disconnect. MCP does not define batch semantics; servers must reject arrays gracefully.
findings.evidence: Server disconnected after batch. Pre-crash response: none
findings.remediation: Validate input type at the JSON-RPC layer: if the parsed value is a list, return -32600 (Invalid Request) immediately without further processing.

findings.title: Protocol crash — oversized method name caused transport failure
findings.description: The server failed to respond to a request with an 8192-byte method name. A robust server should return a JSON-RPC error, not disconnect or crash.
findings.remediation: Add input length validation at the JSON-RPC parsing layer. Return -32700 (parse error) or -32600 (invalid request) for malformed inputs.

findings.title: Protocol crash — deeply nested params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request with 50-level nested params caused the server to disconnect or crash. Deep nesting can cause stack overflows or excessive memory use in recursive JSON parsers.
findings.remediation: Limit JSON object nesting depth at parse time (recommended: ≤32 levels). Return -32700 (Parse error) for inputs exceeding the depth limit.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.
